100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On February 27, 1926, the international stage was marked by significant developments. In the United States, President Calvin Coolidge delivered a speech emphasizing economic stability and the importance of agriculture. In Europe, tensions rose as a series of strikes in Britain highlighted labor unrest, while in Germany, the political landscape continued to shift amid economic challenges. Meanwhile, in the Far East, Japan announced plans for military expansion, stirring concerns among neighboring nations.
